title: selliguea pedunculata
intro: Selliguea pedunculata, also known as Taro fern or Pana pana, is a plant species belonging to the Polypodiaceae family of ferns. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Selliguea pedunculata include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic to aid in the removal of excess fluids from the body. Selliguea pedunculata can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, making it a versatile plant for medicinal purposes.
